title: Florida's chemtrail felony — a law for an activity that doesn't exist
narrative: Florida's Department of Environmental Protection (DEP) received 20,000+ complaints from citizens reporting alleged weather modification/chemtrail activity since Governor Ron DeSantis signed Senate Bill 56 (2025), banning weather modification as a third-degree felony. DEP has found ZERO evidence of any violations. Officials claim citizens are confusing ordinary contrails with secret geoengineering programs.
telemetry: Federal law (50 USC) requires any weather modification operator to file advance reports with NOAA at least 10 days before activities — NOAA Weather Modification Clearinghouse database shows ZERO authorized projects in Florida for 2025
DeSantis publicly mocked constituents raising chemtrail concerns as having 'kooky ideas about blocking the sun' — preceding a law specifically targeting the very practice they feared
DEP's own spokesperson acknowledged the reported phenomenon (contrails) IS real and visible — what officials frame as misidentified is aircraft condensation that's visibly present in Florida skies

No company has been charged, fined, or investigated under SB 56. No equipment has been seized. The law produced a felony category for an activity that demonstrably isn't happening — or isn't being detected
SB 56 creates criminal penalties for weather modification — but federal law already requires disclosure, meaning any legitimate weather modification would already be on record with NOAA
analysis: The law created a felony offense for an activity for which there is no evidence — while actual atmospheric phenomena (contrails) are being visibly emitted by aircraft at unprecedented rates due to increased commercial aviation traffic. Who drafted SB 56, who lobbied for it, and does its existence serve to discredit legitimate citizen observation of the sky — or provide cover for undisclosed atmospheric activity not covered by federal reporting requirements? The NOAA database showing zero authorized projects in Florida is the physical record. The 20,000 complaints are people looking up and seeing something. The gap between those two facts is the story.
verdict: [SIPHONED VERDICT]: Zero authorized weather modification projects in Florida. 20,000 complaints from citizens looking up. The law made it a felony to do something nobody has a permit to do. That's either bureaucratic theater or a tool for discrediting sky-watchers.
